canonical_name: one who will be true to his oath-
such a son as any man, let alone a childless king, would be glad and proud to own
the voice of the Round Table
the voice of the Round Table among the Saxon kings
the daughter of one of the Saxon kings
any other man God ever made
the King Viviane had foreseen would come
King of Avalon and all Britain
a child she had found somewhere and must suckle
one or two of the subject kings
the priests who had so much influence over her
the kind of pious idiots with whom Gwenhwyfar always surrounded herself
the old Queen of North Wales
the old King of North Wales
those who were there last night, when Arthur acknowledged him
the dwarf folk of which tales told that they dwelt beneath the mountains
those old priests who dwelt on Glastonbury
a chapel where they and Taliesin could worship side by side
fanatics like Patricius, who in their pride see the Creator only as the avenging Father of soldiers
the loving Mother of the fields and the earth
the Mother Goddess who could quiet his fear and despair
